# Agent Tool Index

Visual and agent-readable index for ERC-8257 tools across Ethereum, Base, and Abstract.

## Registry

- Registry address: 0x265BB2DBFC0A8165C9A1941Eb1372F349baD2cf1 (same on all chains)
- Chains: Ethereum (1), Base (8453), Abstract (2741)

## Static Files

- /llms.txt (this file)
- /registry-data.js (current snapshot as window.REGISTRY)

## API (live in production)

Base URL: https://agenttoolindex.xyz

- GET /api/tools - list indexed tools (filters: q, status, access, x402, limit)
- GET /api/tools/{tool_id} - single tool record
- POST /api/tools/{tool_id}/can_call - plan whether a caller can invoke a tool
- GET|POST /api/resolve - resolve intent/filter criteria to candidate tools
- GET /api/stats - index statistics
- POST /api/call - invoke a registered tool endpoint (handles the x402 flow)
- GET /openapi.json - OpenAPI 3.1 schema

## Invoking Tools (/api/call)

POST /api/call with {"url": <tool endpoint>, "body": <inputs>}. The host must be in
the registry. A paid tool returns an envelope with status 402 and an `accepts` array
(x402 v1 "exact" scheme). To settle, sign an EIP-3009 transferWithAuthorization for the
USDC `asset` to `payTo` for `maxAmountRequired`, base64-encode the x402 payment payload,
and resend the same request with an `X-PAYMENT` header (or `xPayment` in the body).
Free tools return status 200 with the result directly.

## Tool Records

Each tool record includes: status, creator, metadata URI, access predicate, manifest
verification status (JCS keccak256 hash), x402 detection, endpoint, tags, inputs,
outputs, pricing, and checked_at timestamps.

## Resolve

POST /api/resolve accepts: query, status, access, manifest_status, x402, limit.
Returns scored candidates with invocation hints.

## Call Planning

POST /api/tools/{tool_id}/can_call accepts: wallet, budget_usdc, allow_x402, has_auth.
Returns callable/conditional/not_callable with requirements, blockers, and steps.
